_sources_note: Jira: 3 JQL queries (In Product/Blocked, sprint updated today, status changed today). Slack: #gdc-pod, #gdc-wcpredictor, #gdc-f2p-apps, #core-product, DMs with Vic Dadson, Jessica Dordevic, Ciara Carroll, Colin Brannigan, Chloe Christie, Gabriel Cornoiu. Confluence: GDCU space (0 pages modified today). Channels #gdc-product-design-pod, #gdc-product-led-growth, #gdc-pdmt, #gdc-high-agency not found/accessible. Cross-referenced against today's morning report (08:30 BST).
_morning_delta_note: Key changes since this morning's 08:30 report: (1) GDCU-9007 Cosmo Rotation Block — now DONE (was In Product). (2) GDCU-8795 Refer-a-Friend CTA — now DONE (was In QA). (3) GDCU-8996 WC Predictor Admin Panel — now In Progress with Kelvin Yee (was unassigned Backlog — ownership gap resolved). (4) GDCU-8927 Tabs Component — now In Product (QA passed 11:22, new since morning report). (5) GDCU-8902 Sports Quiz prototype — fixed by Jessica at 12:35 (was broken per morning context). (6) WC Predictor design: Gabriel gave feedback at 08:56 (missing login CTA); Ciara sent Banners V2 via Claude Design at 12:33. (7) Eoin's 4 product questions in #gdc-wcpredictor (from 11/05) still unanswered — flagged as blocking now dev has started.

action_items.action: REVIEW 3 IN PRODUCT TICKETS — BEFORE 14:30 STANDUP. (1) GDCU-8971 Cosmo Spins How-to Videos (Novica) — still open from morning report. 21 Loom videos created. Chloe asked if Eoin is satisfied (14/05). Quick approve or note specific gaps. (2) GDCU-8902 Sports Quiz Design System (Jessica) — prototype broken yesterday, Jessica fixed it today at 12:35 and confirmed Figma updates applied. Mike tagged directly in ticket. Now fully reviewable. (3) GDCU-8927 Tabs Component Image Fields (Harden) — NEW since morning report. QA passed at 11:22 (Shane confirmed), Pernilla (UX) gave visual thumbs-up at 11:53. Chloe asked Erica to do an editor-level test (11:49). Don't wait for Erica — Mike's product review needed in parallel. Test page: qa6.gambling.com/component-test-page. Note: GDCU-9007 (Cosmo Rotation Block) is already DONE — one down from this morning's list.
action_items.morning_status: partially resolved — GDCU-9007 done, GDCU-8927 added, GDCU-8902 now reviewable

action_items.action: WC PREDICTOR — ANSWER EOIN'S 4 PRODUCT QUESTIONS (11/05 #gdc-wcpredictor, now 4 days old, dev STARTED TODAY). Eoin's questions at 11/05 11:12: (1) Golden Boot outright — all World Cup players selectable or a curated shortlist? (2) Knockout games to penalties — if user predicted a draw, do they get points at full time (technically correct score)? (3) Group-stage and knockout score predictions — single predictions screen or separate via the bracket UI? (4) Login CTA — how to handle users who predicted on mobile then visit on desktop? GDCU-8995 (DB/API, Kelvin) and GDCU-8996 (Admin Panel, Kelvin — now assigned) both kicked off today. These questions are actively blocking schema and API design. Post answers in #gdc-wcpredictor immediately.

WC PREDICTOR DESIGN — CLOSE THE LOOP WITH MIGUEL TODAY. Gabriel flagged this morning at 08:56: Figma is missing the sign-in CTA for returning users on the overview screen. Miguel is holding off sending the full prototype to dev until he has Ciara's feedback. Ciara delivered Banners V2 via Claude Design at 12:33 — she's clearly engaged but has not visibly responded on the main design review. Confirm: (1) Has Ciara given Miguel her feedback? (2) Has Miguel added Gabriel's login CTA? GDCU-8995/8996 dev has started — design needs to be locked today. Do not let this drift over the weekend.

JESSICA LINKEDIN RECOMMENDATION — STILL NEEDS HR CHECK. Carried from morning report (high). Mike told Jessica on 14/05: 'I don't have a problem but I need to check with HR first.' Jessica is in a formal redundancy consultation process (~7 days to consultation meeting per her DM). Check with HR now so you can give her a clear answer before end of day today. She's actively working (GDCU-8902 in Product, GDCU-8906 NL Tables) — a quick resolution here supports her engagement during a difficult period.
action_items.morning_status: outstanding — no HR check completed yet visible in data

action_items.action: VIC REDUNDANCY — DATA KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER PLAN. Carried from morning (high). Vic is at risk of redundancy, likely gone within the month. Colin's advice: 'safer to utilise David.' Mike's own 14/05 analysis: Vic holds tracking + insights work (5 of 8 responsibilities not in original role), Bloomreach unconnected, no push reporting, data in silos, no handover plan. Immediate actions for this afternoon / early next week: (1) Agree a structured brain dump plan with Vic — prioritise tracking specs, Bloomreach API wiring, KPI definitions. (2) Align with David Mulero Pino on which requirements to take on. (3) Confirm Tableau access timeline with BI. Vic is listed as an attendee on today's 14:30 standup — confirm she is there and agree 1:1 this week.

WC PREDICTOR ADMIN PANEL (GDCU-8996) — OWNER ASSIGNED, CONFIRM SCOPE. Good news since morning: GDCU-8996 (WC Predictor Admin Panel, HIGH) is now In Progress with Kelvin Yee — the ownership gap flagged in the morning report is resolved. However: confirm Kelvin understands the scope (group game scores, knockout teams/scores, outrights management, point-total triggers on save). He's also on GDCU-8995 (DB/API) simultaneously. Check in with Gabriel/Eoin: is Kelvin double-banked here and does he need support?
action_items.morning_status: partially resolved — owner now assigned (Kelvin), scope confirmation still needed

action_items.action: GDCU-8810 DEVELOPER ACCOUNTS — STILL BLOCKED, DAY 9. Carried from morning report. Apple/Google org-level accounts (Harden, In Progress/Blocked). Primary shortcut: contact RotoWire team to verify if GDC Group org accounts already exist. Raise this explicitly at today's 14:30 standup — it has been blocked for 9 days and the app submission pipeline (Age Gate, Ace Nav — both approved this week) is queued behind it.

GDCU-9006 SEARCH ICON NAV BUG — ASSIGN AT STANDUP. Shane filed this on 14/05 (Backlog, unassigned) and also flagged in #gdc-pod on 13/05 and 14/05. Search icon not appearing in nav on scroll. Possible regression from recent Ace Nav or CWV changes. Assign to appropriate dev at today's 14:30 standup and set Sprint 10 priority.

IUJ ENGAGEMENT — MONITOR ACTIVE FIXES. GDCU-8795 (Refer-a-Friend CTA Blue) is now DONE today (Novica). GDCU-8958 (IUJ Banners, Harden) is In Progress. Two short-term fixes are therefore in motion. Confirm: are these the right levers to move the 30/12,000 level-up conversion? If not, the next decision point (per Mike's note to Colin) is removing the feature. No immediate action today but keep on radar.
action_items.morning_status: progressing — GDCU-8795 done today, GDCU-8958 in progress

action_items.action: NL PAYSAFECARD 404 — HIGH TRAFFIC, REDIRECT DECISION NEEDED. Robin John flagged in #gdc-pod on 14/05 09:54: gambling.com/nl/paysafecard is 404 and is in the top-50 highest-traffic URLs. No response visible from Mike or team. Live SEO and UX issue. Decide: redirect to NL homepage or a relevant NL payment methods page, then ensure a ticket is raised if not already done. NOT in this morning's report — new.

CANDIDATE DEBRIEF — AGREE DECLINE AND NEXT STEPS WITH GABRIEL. Carried from morning (low). Interview on 14/05: Gabriel — 'not hands-on, relies on others, exhausted listening to him.' Mike — 'smart but not advanced enough; could be a junior candidate.' Discussed briefly in DM during/after interview. Formally agree with Gabriel: decline for this role. Given Jessica's situation (redundancy consultation), discuss interim design resource strategy and whether to open a new search.

GDCU-8972 CLAUDE DESIGN IMPROVEMENTS — ASSIGN TO CIARA. Carried from morning (low). Mike confirmed with Chloe on 14/05: 2 SPs, not needed in Sprint planning, Ciara to update design system from stakeholder feedback. Still in Backlog with no assignee. Assign to Ciara and confirm scope (update design system based on Claude Design sessions — Mike gave feedback on spacing vs images on 14/05).

GDCU-8432 GEO LOCATE IMAGES — DE-SCOPE OR ASSIGN DECISION. Carried from morning (medium). Long-standing blocked ticket, unassigned, for geo-located images on Global HP. Sprint 10 planning is starting now — decide today whether to carry this into Sprint 10 or formally de-scope.

Age Gate and Ace Nav both approved this week; GDCU-8969 (create App Store Connect app, Carlos) is next in the pipeline. Raise at today's 14:30 standup.
